steps.title: Standard form $f(x) = a \cdot b^x$ shows $c$ directly as the coefficient
steps.content:
When an exponential function is written as $f(x) = a(b)^x$, we have:
$$f(0) = a(b)^0 = a$$
The coefficient $a$ equals $f(0) = c$ directly, without computation needed. Any exponent shift like $b^{x+1}$ or $b^{x-1}$ requires calculation.

steps.title: Identify the standard form choice
steps.content:
Scanning the choices:
• Choice A: has $b^{x+1}$ (shifted)
• Choice B: $f(x) = 33(1.5)^x$ has standard form
• Choice C: has $b^{x-1}$ (shifted)
• Choice D: has $b^{x-2}$ (shifted)

Only Choice B has no exponent shift, so $f(0) = 33(1.5)^0 = 33$ and the coefficient 33 is the value of $c$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
